articles.title: Temporal Derivative Soft-Sensing and Reconstructing Solar Radiation and Heat Flux from Common Environmental Sensors
articles.description: While we proved this works using the FiaOS architecture, the method is hardware independent. The main goal of this work is to release a high-performance, open-source software library built for professional developers. By sharing this code globally, we aim to upgrade the abilities of millions of current and future smart devices. This library allows standard electronics to go beyond simple readings and unlock a deeper understanding of environmental physics. By calculating complex energy flows using common sensors, we open a new frontier of applications, from precision engineering to automated energy management, making advanced weather science accessible to engineers.

articles.title: The Suspected Hierarchy of Human Intentions and Brain Signals
articles.description: Exploring how the brain organizes movement signals in a hierarchy of purpose, strength, and frequency

articles.title: The Use of Evapotranspiration Algorithm in Umbrella Project and the Penman-Monteith Equation
articles.description: A comprehensive exploration of the evapotranspiration algorithm and Penman-Monteith equation implementation in the Umbrella Project autonomous irrigation system
articles.author: Neksha DeSilva
